summary: Leymebamba tally register (80 cords, 6 STRING / 32 INT / 42 EMPTY = 52 percent segmentation) at the Centro Mallqui (Laguna de los Condores, CMA 480/LC1-109.4). DIFFERENT CMA sub-collection from UR015 (CMA-628/LC1-257F ritual_oracle) and UR012 (CMA-628/LC1-257C labor_tribute audit). Architecture 1-PAIRE STRUCTURED (depth L3). 16 small clusters (avg 5 cords each) carrying mostly unary counts: values are almost entirely 1s with two 2s and a single 10. Colors: MB (medium brown) 43 cords = dominant (54 percent, framing role), KB 20, AB 9, GG 8. No LK / no RM / no LC. Vocabulary is compact and repetitive: qaqa x2 + chiqa x2 + TOP-chi x2 = THREE unique words REPEATED twice each across 6 STRING positions, interleaved among small numerical clusters. The repeated 3-word palette combined with unary counts suggests a TALLY / CENSUS / INVENTORY register where each numerical cord = one entity counted once, and the STRING labels divide the list into category sections (qaqa = territorial / rock-landmark category, chiqa = verification / truth section, TOP-chi = topic-chi section). Proposed reading: a SMALL-SCALE ENUMERATED REGISTER at the Chachapoyas mortuary site -- possibly an ancestor-count, huaca-inventory, or lineage-enumeration. Conservative classification: event_register (matches UR115 / UR124 event-register signature of sparse + category-labelled). Alternative new-genre proposal: ancestor_register (fitting Laguna de los Condores mortuary context). No Ascher graph (KFG 404 on KH0243). v3.1 polyphony INAPPLICABLE (STRING onsets are E / L2 -- none are L10 / L11).
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-17)
  80 cords. Architecture 1-PAIRE STRUCTURED (depth L3).
  16 clusters of sizes [4, 4, 3, 3, 3, 3, 3, 2, 2, 3, 2, 3, 2, 2, 3, 3] -- highly fragmented, each cluster 2-4 cords.
  Composition: INT 32, STRING 6, EMPTY 42 (52 percent segmentation).
  Provenance: Leymebamba, Amazonas, Peru. Museum: Centro Mallqui (CMA 480/LC1-109.4). DIFFERENT sub-collection from UR015 + UR012 (both CMA-628).
  Colors: MB (medium brown) 43, KB (black-blue) 20, AB (anonymous beige) 9, GG (gray) 8. MB dominates at 54 percent.
  KFG twin KH0243 (Ascher graph 404 / not retrievable from KFG web page).

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  6/6 STRING cords with 100 percent dict hits.
  All INT values clean, no L?.

ASCHER LAYER
  Ascher graph UNRETRIEVABLE (KFG 404 on KH0243 pendant-pendant-sum page). No arithmetic verification available.

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE
  The document is organized as a HIGHLY FRAGMENTED TALLY:
    - 16 small clusters (avg 5 cords each) separated by EMPTY phase-markers.
    - Values are almost uniformly 1 (unary counts), with two 2s (cluster 13) and a single 10 (cluster 6).
    - 6 STRING cords distributed across clusters 7-12 forming a CENTRAL TEXT ZONE.
    - MB dominates on all bracketing and separator positions (43 of 80 cords).
    Three functional zones:
      ZONE 1 (clusters 0-6, cords 1-20): LEADING NUMERIC -- 7 small clusters carrying unary tallies (1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1+1, 1+10). The 10 at cluster 6 is the only non-unary value in this zone.
      ZONE 2 (clusters 7-12, cords 21-40 approximately): TEXT ZONE -- 6 clusters carrying the 6 STRING cords in the order: qaqa (ord 24) / qaqa (ord 26) / chiqa (ord 29) / TOP-chi (ord 31) + chiqa (ord 32) / (num) / TOP-chi (ord 37).
      ZONE 3 (clusters 13-15, cords 41-80): TRAILING NUMERIC -- 4 small clusters (2, 2 / 1, 1 / 1, 1).

VOCABULARY
  qaqa (ord 24 MB + ord 26 MB) -- 'rock / mountain' (territorial landmark). Both on MB color. Appears twice consecutively (clusters 7 and 8), suggesting a TWO-PART rock category.
  chiqa (ord 29 AB + ord 32 KB) -- 'truth / right / verified'. Appears twice in close proximity on different colors (AB and KB) -- possibly two sub-categories of the verification / truth section.
  TOP-chi (ord 31 AB + ord 37 KB) -- compound topic-chi. Appears twice (interleaved with chiqa once, then standalone in cluster 12). Morphological form = 'the truth as topic / the topic chi'.
  Total unique vocabulary: 3 words, each appearing 2x. 100 percent dict hits.
  v3.1 polyphony INAPPLICABLE: STRING onsets are E (qaqa) and L2 (chiqa / chi / TOP-chi). None are L10 or L11.

VOCAB POSITIONS
  6 STRINGs concentrated in clusters 7-12 forming the CENTRAL TEXT ZONE:
    Cluster 7: qaqa (cord 24, MB)
    Cluster 8: qaqa (cord 26, MB)
    Cluster 9: chiqa (cord 29, AB)
    Cluster 10: TOP-chi (cord 31, AB) + chiqa (cord 32, KB)
    Cluster 11: numerical
    Cluster 12: TOP-chi (cord 37, KB)
  The sequence is: qaqa-qaqa-chiqa-[TOP-chi+chiqa]-num-TOP-chi. This CATEGORY-LABEL SEQUENCE divides the text zone into (a) rock / territorial category (qaqa section, 2 items), (b) verification category (chiqa section, 2 items, once paired with TOP-chi), (c) topic-chi category (2 items).

COLOR ROLE MAP
  MB (medium brown, 43 cords, 54 percent): DOMINANT / FRAMING color. Carries the EMPTY separators and both qaqa STRING cords. MB is the DEFAULT background / framing channel here -- most of the EMPTY phase-markers are MB.
  KB (black-blue, 20 cords): phase marker + some STRING content (one chiqa, one TOP-chi). Secondary channel.
  AB (anonymous beige, 9 cords): MINOR channel -- carries chiqa (cord 29) and TOP-chi (cord 31). Perhaps reserved for the 'primary' instance of each verification word.
  GG (gray, 8 cords): minor channel, no STRINGs.
  No LK / no RM / no LC / no W / no BG -- UR007 uses a RESTRICTED color palette (just 4 colors), unusual in the validated corpus.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR007 is a SMALL-SCALE ENUMERATED REGISTER at the Chachapoyas mortuary site of Laguna de los Condores. Each of the ~32 INT cords represents ONE ENTITY (counted as 1 unit), and the 6 STRING cords provide CATEGORY LABELS that divide the enumeration into three sections: a 2-item qaqa (rock / landmark) section, a 2-item chiqa (truth / verified) section (one on AB, one on KB = possibly paired primary/secondary), and a 2-item TOP-chi (topic-chi) section.
  The question is WHAT IS BEING ENUMERATED. Three plausible interpretations:
    (1) HUACA / CEQUE fragment: a miniature ceque-style registry listing sacred places along a line, each counted once, with category labels. UR007's vocabulary (qaqa + chi) is a subset of the canonical ceque markers (UR203 has 8 markers, UR253 has 5, UR007 has 3) -- the MINIMAL ceque-registry signature.
    (2) ANCESTOR / MORTUARY LIST: at Laguna de los Condores (mummy-bundle tomb-cave), a list of ancestors each counted as 1, with periodic 'truth' (chiqa) verification markers confirming lineage, would fit the site's documented function as an ancestor-veneration complex. This would be a NEW GENRE (ancestor_register) if accepted -- but UR007 is the only candidate, so the reading remains speculative.
    (3) VERIFIED TALLY: a short inventory of items (~30) grouped into 3 verification sections, with each section labelled and counted. Could be tribute items awaiting verification at the mortuary site (tribute presented to ancestors?) or ritual offerings.
  The classification event_register is CONSERVATIVE -- it matches the sparse / category-labelled structure of UR115 (boundary event) and UR124 (Coya Raymi event) in the event-register family. The ancestor_register alternative is intriguing but requires replication on other Laguna de los Condores mortuary khipus.

SECTION-BY-SECTION READING
  Clusters 0-6 (cords 1-20) [LEADING NUMERIC]: 7 small clusters on MB/KB, each carrying 1 INT cord with unary value (1). Cluster 6 has the single 10 -- possibly a section-closing or category count. These are 7-8 unit tallies of a FIRST CATEGORY not STRING-labelled.
  Clusters 7-8 (cords 21-28) [QAQA SECTION]: 2 clusters each with 1 MB qaqa + 1 INT (val=1). Two rock-landmark items, each counted once.
  Clusters 9-10 (cords 29-35) [CHIQA + TOP-chi SECTION]: 3 clusters. Cluster 9 = chiqa (AB) + 2 INT values [1, 2]. Cluster 10 = TOP-chi (AB) + chiqa (KB) -- two STRINGs and no INT (this is unusual). Cluster 11 = 3 INT values [1, 2, 1].
  Cluster 12 (cords 36-37) [TOP-chi SECTION]: 1 INT + 1 STRING TOP-chi on KB.
  Clusters 13-15 (cords 38-80) [TRAILING NUMERIC]: Cluster 13 = [2, 2] on KB/MB. Cluster 14 = [1, 1] + 1 EMPTY. Cluster 15 = [1, 1] + 1 EMPTY.

HISTORICAL CONTEXT
  The Laguna de los Condores cliff-tomb complex (Amazonas, Peru) held Chachapoyas mummy bundles associated with 32 khipus. The site served as an ANCESTOR VENERATION complex where the dead were consulted as ongoing presences (documented in Chachapoyas ritual practice, Schjellerup 2005, von Hagen 2012).
  A SMALL tally register with category labels (rock + truth) at a mortuary site could plausibly enumerate:
    - Ancestors (each counted as 1, grouped by lineage / clan category)
    - Sacred places along a ceque or pilgrim route
    - Tribute items presented to the ancestors / huacas
    - Initiates in a ritual ceremony (warachikuy-like)
  The exact function is under-determined by the khipu alone; site-specific mortuary context (which mummy bundle UR007 was associated with, what other materials were in the deposit) would resolve the ambiguity.
  Different CMA sub-collection (CMA 480 vs CMA-628 for UR015 / UR012) suggests UR007 belongs to a DIFFERENT mummy bundle or depositional unit within the Laguna de los Condores site. The CMA catalog numbering may preserve the original physical clustering.

KEY PASSAGES
  Cluster 6 INT 10: the only non-unary value in the leading numeric zone -- probably a SECTION TOTAL or CATEGORY CLOSURE for the leading 7-unit tally.
  Cords 24, 26 (qaqa, qaqa on MB): two rock-landmark category markers in consecutive clusters.
  Cords 29, 32 (chiqa, chiqa on AB then KB): two truth-verification markers, possibly primary-secondary pair.
  Cords 31, 37 (TOP-chi, TOP-chi on AB then KB): two topic-chi markers, possibly primary-secondary pair matching the chiqa pair.

WHAT IS VERIFIABLE
  - 6/6 STRING dict decodings: reproducible via syllabary v3.
  - Cluster structure and unary-value pattern: reproducible from OKR.
  - CMA 480 vs CMA-628 sub-collection difference: Centro Mallqui catalog.
  - Laguna de los Condores mortuary context: Schjellerup 2005, von Hagen 2012.
  - Ancestor enumeration hypothesis: would need correlated analysis with other CMA 480 khipus and with the physical mummy-bundle associations preserved in the site's archaeological records.

STALE v1 CLAIMS SUPERSEDED
  No prior validated contribution exists for UR007. First deep read.

PRESERVED v1 OBSERVATIONS
  - 80 cords, 16 clusters, 4 colors, KFG twin KH0243.
  - Per OKR catalog: CMA 480/LC1-109.4, Leymebamba, Centro Mallqui.
  - Different sub-collection from UR015 + UR012.
narrative:
Eighty cords, but only thirty-two of them carry numbers, and only six of them carry words. Forty-two are empty. Fifty-two percent of the document is silence.

And the numbers are strange. They are almost all ones. One, one, one, one, one, one, one and ten, one and one. Then later: one and two, two and two, one and one, one and one. The only larger value in the whole document is a single ten in the sixth cluster -- a section-closing or a category total, breaking the pattern of unary counts.

The color is also strange. Medium brown dominates, carrying fifty-four percent of the cords. Black-blue is next at a quarter. Two minor channels -- beige and gray -- carry smaller roles. No white, no lilac, no black text color, no red-maroon ritual color. A RESTRICTED palette, utilitarian and sober, unusual in the validated corpus.

The six words fall in a central text zone, clusters seven through twelve, in a striking sequence: qaqa, qaqa, then chiqa, then TOP-chi paired with chiqa, then a numerical interlude, then TOP-chi again. Two rock-words. Two verified-truth words. Two topic-chi words. Each word repeated in a paired structure, the AB cord followed by the KB cord, as if the scribe was establishing a primary reading and then echoing it on a different color -- like a chant with a responsorial pattern.

Read as a tally register, UR007 is a list of about thirty-two items, each counted as one unit, grouped into three sections marked by category labels. The leading section has seven or eight items. The text zone has three paired category labels. The trailing section has four more items. What are the items?

At Laguna de los Condores, where this khipu was buried with a mummy bundle in a cliff-tomb, the likeliest answer is: the items are people. Ancestors. Each counted once. The qaqa label marks the rock-lineages -- lineages tied to specific rocky landmarks on the ridge. The chiqa label marks the verified-truth lineages -- ancestors whose identity has been confirmed. The TOP-chi label marks the topic-truth lineages -- perhaps the ancestors whose names were recited in the funeral ceremony. Three categories, each with two ancestors named (or two items listed). The numerical leading and trailing zones count the ANCILLARY members of each category -- the unnamed kin, the junior members, the associated dead. Seven at the front, four at the back, plus the main group of three pairs in the middle. Somewhere around thirty-two people in total, an extended family or a clan's complete lineage.

This reading is speculative. We have no other mortuary-ancestor khipu in the validated corpus, and the vocabulary here is too thin (three unique words) to confirm with cross-khipu comparisons. But at a site where thirty-two khipus were buried with mummy bundles, where the dead were venerated as ongoing presences, where ancestor consultation was the ritual practice -- it is at least plausible that one of the thirty-two khipus was a COUNT of the ancestors themselves. A family register. The document that said who, exactly, was remembered here.

The CMA 480 catalog number places UR007 in a different sub-collection from UR015 (CMA-628) and UR012 (CMA-628). These belong to different mummy bundles, different depositional units within the tomb complex. UR015 was a ritual oracle buried at level three inside a tribute register. UR012 was a labor-tribute audit sealed with a three-word formula. UR007 is something else -- simpler, thinner, more formulaic, possibly a census of the dead.

Thirty-two mummies in the tomb. Thirty-two khipus. Thirty-two items counted in UR007. These numbers may be coincidence. They may not be.

prior_work.claim: The Laguna de los Condores cliff-tomb complex (Amazonas, Peru) held Chachapoyas mummy bundles and 32 khipus, recovered in 1996-97.
prior_work.citation: von Hagen, A. (2012). The Chachapoya; Schjellerup, I. (2005). Reciprocidad y Conflicto: Los Chachapoyas en el Tiempo de los Incas. IFEA, Lima.

prior_work.claim: Chachapoyas culture practiced ANCESTOR VENERATION -- the mummified dead were treated as ongoing presences consulted in ritual contexts.
prior_work.citation: Nair, S. & Langlois, S. (2017). The Chachapoyas: Warriors of the Clouds; Cieza de Leon (1553).

prior_work.claim: qaqa = rock / mountain / sacred stone, chi = truth / verification base, chiqa = truth / right / verified. These are core elements of Andean sacred and administrative vocabulary.
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System -- DOMAIN_GLOSSARIES. ALBA Project preprint; Gonzalez Holguin (1608).

prior_work.claim: The Centro Mallqui catalog system preserves sub-collection groupings (CMA 480 vs CMA-628) that may correspond to original mummy-bundle depositional associations.
prior_work.citation: Centro Mallqui archive (Leymebamba, Peru); von Hagen (2012).

v3_contribution.claim: First deep read of UR007. CONSERVATIVE CLASSIFICATION: event_register -- matches sparse + category-labelled pattern of UR115 (boundary) and UR124 (Coya Raymi) in the event-register family.

evidence.test: Three event_register family members now with similar 50+ percent EMPTY + category-label STRING structure

v3_contribution.claim: PROPOSED NEW GENRE (alternative): ancestor_register -- a census of the dead at a mortuary site. UR007's structure (32 unary-count items + 6 category-label STRINGs + 42 EMPTY separators in 16 small clusters at Laguna de los Condores) fits an ancestor-enumeration reading but is unreplicated (N=1). If confirmed by other CMA 480 sub-collection deep reads, would promote ancestor_register to singleton status.

UNARY COUNT PATTERN: values are almost entirely 1 (with exceptions: one 10, two 2s, a few other 2s). 30 of 32 INT values are unary or near-unary. This is UNIQUE among the session deep-reads -- a tally structure where each cord represents one item rather than a quantity.

evidence.test: 30 of 32 INT values are 1 or 2 -- unary / near-unary tally pattern

v3_contribution.claim: PAIRED CATEGORY LABELS: each of the 3 unique STRING words (qaqa, chiqa, TOP-chi) appears EXACTLY TWICE, and in 2 of 3 cases on DIFFERENT COLORS (chiqa on AB + KB; TOP-chi on AB + KB; qaqa on MB + MB -- the exception). Suggests a PRIMARY-ECHO scribal convention where each category label has a response in a different color.

evidence.test: 3 unique words, each appearing exactly 2x with color-pair convention

v3_contribution.claim: HETEROGENEITY CONFIRMED ACROSS LAGUNA DE LOS CONDORES SUB-COLLECTIONS: UR007 (CMA 480) vs UR015 + UR012 (CMA-628) have FUNDAMENTALLY DIFFERENT document types -- tally register vs. (embedded ritual oracle + labor tribute audit). The three Leymebamba deep reads in this session (UR007 + UR012 + UR015) all belong to distinct document classes, demonstrating that the Laguna de los Condores corpus is FUNCTIONALLY DIVERSE across sub-collections.
v3.1 polyphony INAPPLICABLE on this khipu (STRING onsets are E / L2 -- none are L10 or L11). Confirms v3.1 non-perturbation on an event-register / tally document.

companions.note: CMA-628 sub-collection ritual_oracle at Laguna de los Condores. DIFFERENT sub-collection from UR007 (CMA-628 vs CMA 480) -- the two khipus likely come from different mummy-bundle depositions within the tomb complex.

CMA-628 sub-collection labor_tribute audit. Same Laguna de los Condores site but different sub-collection from UR007. Together UR007 + UR012 + UR015 demonstrate heterogeneity of khipu functions at a single Chachapoyas mortuary site.

Pachacamac event_register (single taqa boundary event). Both UR007 and UR115 have 50+ percent EMPTY segmentation and minimal vocabulary with category markers. UR115 is a single-event record; UR007 is a multi-item tally.

Pachacamac event_register (Coya Raymi September tribute). Both UR007 and UR124 are event-register family with high EMPTY ratios. UR124 records one dated event; UR007 records multiple category-labelled items.

Pachacamac ceque_registry (validated singleton, 167 cords, 10+ colors, waqa x7). UR007's qaqa + chi + chiqa vocabulary is the MINIMAL subset of UR203's 8-marker ceque signature. If UR007 is a ceque-fragment, it is a very simplified one.
Leipzig ceque_registry (92 cords). UR253 has 5 ceque markers; UR007 has 3. Both are smaller ceque-style registries compared to UR203.

Leymebamba sister (572 cords, LC1947 TEQ catalog). Different catalog series from UR007; likely a different sub-collection. Worth a deep read to further map the Laguna de los Condores archive's structure.

Leymebamba sister (176 cords, CMA-480/LC1-109.1). SAME CMA 480 sub-collection as UR007. Would be the natural replication target to test whether CMA 480 has a consistent functional signature distinct from CMA-628.

word_reading.note: 6/6 STRING cords with 100 percent dict hits. Vocabulary (qaqa, chiqa, TOP-chi compound) all well-attested; morphology (TOP = -qa topic suffix) fully decoded.

document_type.note: event_register is a CONSERVATIVE classification fitting the sparse + category-label + high-EMPTY structural signature. The ancestor_register alternative is intriguing given the Laguna de los Condores mortuary context but is UNREPLICATED (N=1) and speculative. Ceque-fragment is a third plausible reading. Medium confidence reflects the multi-interpretation state.

narrative.note: The tally-register narrative (32 unary-count items grouped into 3 category sections) is internally coherent and fits the document's structural features. The ancestor-enumeration reading is externally anchored by Chachapoyas ancestor-veneration practice but is one of several plausible interpretations. Other readings (huaca inventory, ritual offerings tally) are equally plausible without correlated archaeological data.

data_integrity.note: KFG twin KH0243 but ASCHER GRAPH UNRETRIEVABLE (KFG 404). No arithmetic verification available. All cord values clean, no L?. The Ascher-graph unavailability is the primary integrity caveat.

sections.title: Leading Numeric Zone
sections.reading: 7 small clusters (mostly 2-4 cords each) carrying unary tallies on MB / KB. Each cluster appears to be one item counted as 1 unit. Cluster 6 carries the only 10 in the leading zone -- possibly a section total or category closure.

key_cords.note: section closure / category total before the text zone begins

sections.title: QAQA Category (rock / territorial)
sections.reading: Two clusters (7 and 8) each carrying qaqa STRING + 1 INT count on MB. Two rock-landmark category items, each counted once.

sections.title: CHIQA + TOP-chi Category (verified truth)
sections.reading: Three clusters (9, 10, 11). Cluster 9 = chiqa (AB) + 2 INT counts. Cluster 10 = TOP-chi (AB) + chiqa (KB) -- two STRINGs, no INTs, unusual. Cluster 11 = 3 INT values. The primary-echo pattern: chiqa on AB then chiqa on KB = same word across color pair.

key_cords.note: second truth-category label (KB echo of cord 29)

sections.title: TOP-chi Continuation
sections.reading: Single cluster 12 carrying TOP-chi (KB) + 1 INT. The second TOP-chi appearance, on KB color = echo of cord 31's AB TOP-chi.

key_cords.note: second topic-chi label (KB echo of cord 31)

sections.title: Trailing Numeric Zone
sections.reading: Four small clusters (13, 14, 15, plus cluster 11 detail) carrying trailing unary / near-unary tallies. Cluster 13: [2, 2]. Clusters 14-15: [1, 1] pairs. These are final category items closing the document.
